ops.title: Release cc-transcript scratchpad subcommand (13.1.0) — deferred on concurrent-session release conflict
ops.description:
The `scratchpad` subcommand + tests are DONE and reviewed (finder+refuter+re-attack; the one dot-traversal residual fixed; 16 scoped tests green). My scratchpad work is committed in git object **1402844e** ("feat(cli): scratchpad subcommand (13.1.0)") and present in the working tree (cc_transcript/cli.py, tests/test_cli.py, pyproject.toml at 13.1.0). It was NOT pushed.

Deferred because the cc-transcript working copy is a concurrent-session hotbed: `main@origin` is another session's `lspkztyk` (fix(api), already pushed); there are live `p4-api`/`p2-smallmods-backup` branches; and **another session is preparing its OWN 13.1.0 release** (a `docs(changelog): 13.1.0 — dispatch heartbeat ledger` head, unrelated to scratchpad). Pushing a `v13.1.0` tag with only the scratchpad change would clobber their in-flight 13.1.0. Two `ccx vcs ship` attempts also left a colocated git/jj desync (stray empty `zysuslom` with my message; git HEAD ahead of jj `main`) — do not force through it.

To finish cleanly (coordinate with the concurrent session first): decide the version (their 13.1.0 may need to become 13.2.0, or scratchpad folds into their 13.1.0). Then, from a FRESH clone of origin to avoid the shared-working-copy tangle: apply the 3-file scratchpad diff (extract from git 1402844e), bump the agreed version, commit, tag, push → release-pypi.yml fires on the `v*` tag. After PyPI lands: `env -u UV_EXCLUDE_NEWER uvx cc-transcript@latest scratchpad --help` to smoke it, then the live codex-ask scratchpad-adoption smoke (a bare codex-ask in a scratchpad-bearing session lands its files in a per-call subdir there). codex-ask's mktemp fallback means nothing is blocked in the meantime.

ops.text: cc-transcript scratchpad subcommand released to PyPI at an agreed version (coordinated with the concurrent 13.1.0 release)
Live codex-ask scratchpad-adoption smoke passes against the released version
